channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: Dormant GitHub Accounts Help Attackers Blend In While Mapping Corporate Orgs
item.description:
Datadog Security Labs is warning of "several overlapping campaigns" that are systematically enumerating corporate GitHub organizations, repositories, and user accounts through the GitHub API.
"Operat...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New GigaWiper Windows Backdoor Bundles Disk Wiping, Fake Ransomware, and Spyware
item.description: Microsoft has taken apart a destructive Windows backdoor it calls GigaWiper. What stands out is how it is built: not one tool but three older destructive programs bolted into one, offered as commands ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Helix vishing group emerges in SharePoint data theft attacks
item.description: A new data-extortion group called Helix is using identity-focused tactics such as voice phishing (vishing), device code phishing, and multi-factor authentication (MFA) abuse to steal data from SharePo...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft expects more Windows security updates from AI-discovered flaws
item.description: Microsoft says Windows users should expect to see an increase in security updates as the company increasingly relies on artificial intelligence to discover vulnerabilities in its codebase. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: npm 12 Disables Install Scripts by Default to Reduce Supply Chain Risk
item.description: GitHub has officially announced the release of npm version 12 with install scripts disabled by default, along with deprecating granular access tokens (GATs) designed to bypass two-factor authenticatio...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ThreatsDay: Cloud Bucket Hijacking, Windows LPE Chain, Global Fraud Bust + 17 More Stories
item.description:
Most security mess starts as admin work. A link gets clicked. A tool gets trusted. A bucket name gets reused. A setting stays loose because nobody wants to touch it.
This week is full of that kind of...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: New Forg365 phishing platform uses AI to target Microsoft 365 accounts
item.description: A new phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operation called Forg365 focuses on stealing Microsoft 365 accounts by combining adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) and device code methods with AI-assisted lure genera...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: As Global Conflicts Go Digital, Businesses Need Wartime Gameplans
item.description: The fate of a Ukrainian tax software company shows how modern cyberwarfare can claim casualties far beyond the battlefield, and how businesses across the ocean still need to protect themselves....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: The Hidden Security Risks of Reduced Summer IT Coverage
item.description: Security operations don't slow down when IT teams take vacation, but staffing levels often do. Kaseya explains how AI-driven automation can help organizations maintain consistent security operations a...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Gateways Offer Attackers the Keys to the Kingdom
item.description: A cryptomining incident highlights how AI gateways can provide access to AI models, cloud infrastructure, and identity and access management (IAM) data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI Attacks Move in Minutes. Join This Webinar on Building a Defense That Keeps Up
item.description: AI has changed how fast attacks move. Work that once took an attacker days now takes minutes. Using models like Mythos, attackers write tailored bait, pick targets, test what lands, and jump to the ne...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft to retire the OWA Light client in Exchange Server
item.description: Microsoft has announced plans to disable Outlook Web Access (OWA) Light, the lightweight version of the Outlook Web App email client, in a future Exchange Server update. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Summer of Clearinghouses
item.description: Everyone seems to have announced a clearinghouse over the past few weeks. We did too. Ours is called Athena, and the main thing that sets it apart is that it was already real and running when we annou...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new ransomware family called GodDamn that employs the PoisonX kernel driver to neutralize security software as part of its defense evasion strategy.
According...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 'GodDamn' Ransomware Uses BYOVD to Smite US Companies
item.description: Microsoft signed a malicious kernel driver, and now it's being used to kill security software in ransomware attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Police arrests 5,800 suspects in global anti-fraud crackdown
item.description: Law enforcement agencies have arrested 5,811 suspects and seized $293 million in illicit assets in a global anti-fraud operation spanning 97 countries. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Patches RoguePlanet Defender Flaw That Can Grant SYSTEM Privileges
item.description:
Microsoft has released security updates for a Defender vulnerability known as RoguePlanet, nearly a month after details of the flaw became public.
The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-50656 (CVSS s...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: European Organizations Have a Collaboration Security Confidence Gap
item.description: A new survey shows security leaders have an inflated sense of safety regarding their collaboration tools and platforms....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AssuranceAmerica data breach exposes records of 6.9 million drivers
item.description: American insurance company AssuranceAmerica has disclosed a data breach impacting nearly 7 million drivers after attackers gained access to its systems earlier this year. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Meta's New AI Image Tool Lets Others Use Your Public Instagram Photos in AI Images
item.description:
Meta has announced that its new artificial intelligence (AI) model Muse Image lets people use public Instagram posts and reels to generate AI content, and it's enabled by default.
"You can also @-men...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft patches RoguePlanet Defender zero-day vulnerability
item.description: Microsoft has released a security patch to address a Defender zero-day vulnerability known as "RoguePlanet," disclosed after the June 2026 Patch Tuesday. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
item.description:
Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead.
That is the finding in a proof-of-concept published Wednesday by ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GhostApproval Symlink Flaws Could Let Malicious Repos Run Code in AI Coding Agents
item.description: Researchers at Wiz found that a flaw in six popular AI coding assistants lets a booby-trapped code project quietly take control of a developer's computer. The assistant asks permission to edit one har...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Fake 7-Zip Installers Turn Devices Into Residential Proxy Nodes
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new threat actor dubbed Lurking Lizard that has been operating an end-to-end malicious residential proxy business using an infrastructure comprisi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Mexico's New Cyber Plan Faces Its First Real Test
item.description: The Latin American nation's cybersecurity plan — still in the expansion phase — has to survive its own knockout round during the FIFA World Cup....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Mount Royal University confirms breach as hackers claim attack
item.description: Mount Royal University in Calgary says hackers stole and then deleted data from its file storage systems after breaching the university's network. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Lone Attacker Uses AI to Breach AWS Cloud Environment in 72 Hours
item.description: The attacker exploited AI workflows, chained cloud weaknesses, and stolen credentials to extort a large Amazon customer....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Fake Paysafe, Skrill SDKs on NPM and PyPi steal credentials
item.description: Malicious packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) and the Python Package Index (PyPI) delivered stealer malware to developers and users of Paysafe, Skrill, and Neteller payment applications. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Hackers exploit Roundcube flaw to spy on academic researchers
item.description: A China-linked threat cluster has been exploiting vulnerable Roundcube servers at U.S. and Canadian universities to steal credentials and deploy backdoor malware. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Coding Agents Found Triggering Endpoint Security Rules Built to Catch Attackers
item.description:
Sophos looked at a week of its own endpoint data and found that AI coding agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenAI Codex are setting off detection rules written to catch human intruders.
The ag...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
